events: June nonfarm payrolls released today 8:30am ET: +57k vs +113k expected; unemployment 4.2%
US markets CLOSED Fri Jul 3 (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 falls on Saturday)
CPI release (June inflation) Tue Jul 14 8:30am ET
rates: steady at 3.50-3.75%; Sep hike probability fell to ~51% after weak jobs print
note: SPY is 7.9% above its 200-day average — the market is in an uptrend, not a crisis. However, the S&P 500 P/E near 27 sits at the 89th-91st historical percentile, meaning the market is expensive by long-run standards. All three candidates with active theses (AAPL, MSFT, V) trade above our base fair values. Rates remain elevated at 3.5-3.75% and a September hike is still a coin-flip after today's soft jobs report. Two significant macro events ahead — CPI on Jul 14 and the FOMC decision Jul 28-29 — could move prices materially. Hold cash; demand a larger margin of safety than normal before buying.
